canonical_definition: The Wilcoxon signed-rank test is presented in the provided literature as a non-parametric statistical test. Its documented application is to determine whether performance differences between systems, such as a proposed model and its baselines, are significant across multiple tasks. One paper explicitly states its use for this purpose, noting it is a "non-parametric test" ("Anecdoctoring: Automated Red-Teaming Across Language and Place"). Another study applies this test in the context of the Tabular classification task. Across the available data, this test is consistently framed as a tool for statistical significance testing of model performance.
papers: Agent-to-Agent Theory of Mind: Testing Interlocutor Awareness among Large Language Models
Anecdoctoring: Automated Red-Teaming Across Language and Place
Sparse Activation Editing for Reliable Instruction Following in Narratives

definitions.text: Non-parametric statistical test used to determine whether performance differences between ACING and baselines are significant across tasks.
definitions.paper: Anecdoctoring: Automated Red-Teaming Across Language and Place

relationships.paper: Mixture of In-Context Prompters for Tabular PFNs
